para_trans: On the creation of new Public Accounts-09 and Public Accounts-02 by splitting the Public Accounts Executive of the Revenue and Forest Department.
Government of Maharashtra
Revenue and Forest Department
Government Corrigendum No: - OFM-2024 / Q. No.96 / Pr. 1 (E),
Madam Kama Marg, Martyr Rajguru Chowk,
Mantralaya, Mumbai - 400 032.
Date: April 10, 2026.
Read: - 1) Revenue and Revenue Department, Government Circular No: - OFM-2024 / P.No.96 / E, D. 10/07/2025.
2) Revenue & Forest Department, Office Order No: - OFM-2024 / Q. No.96 / E,
3) Revenue & Forest Department, Office Order No: - OFM-2024 / Q. No.96 / E,
4) Revenue & Forest Department, Office Order No: - OFM-2024 / Q. No.96 / E,
5) Department of Revenue & Forest, Office Order No: - OFM-2024 / P.No.96 / E,
the Government Corrigendum

The Table of Contents of Office in the Revenue and Forest Departments Reference No. 1 has been published by the Government Circular No. In this table of contents, Reference No. Amendments have been made under 02, 03, 04B05. The "Public Accounts" office in the Sub-Division of Revenue, Annexure A to this Schedule is being divided into "Public Accounts-01" and "Public Accounts-02" offices for administrative reasons. Subjects in these worksheets are in the order of the appendix. (Annexure) 02. The said Government WAH 7/5/5 of the Government of Maharashtra. The 11919195119.205.11 code is 202604101503057619. The certificate is being digitally signed and authenticated.
By order and in the name of the Governor of Maharashtra, DNYANADEV a.
enorme LAXMAN SUL Sisson
Joint Secretary, Government of Maharashtra.
Pr. - 1. Ma. Private Secretary to the Minister (Revenue / Forests / Relief & Rehabilitation).
2. Ma. Private Secretary to the Minister of State (Revenue / Forests / Relief & Rehabilitation).
3. Additional Chief Secretary (Revenue, Stamp Duty and Registration. Officer on Special Duty, Revenue & Forest Department, Mantralaya, Mumbai - Yoo 022.
4. A.M.S. (Forests) / P.S. (Relief and Rehabilitation) / A. Personal Assistant to M.S. (Avri), Revenue & Forest Department, Mantralaya, Mumbai.
5. All Divisional Commissioners / All Collectors.
6. All Working Groups, Revenue & Forest Department, Mantralaya, Mumbai-400 032.
7. Selection.
Pairing - A
table_trans: Subjects of the workshop as per the schedule

1) All matters relating to the Public Accounts Committee, including: a) Appropriation Accounts and Financial Accounts (SAMIR Accounts, Finance Accounts) b) USAR of Losses and Misappropriation c) Drafting of Audit Reports (Draft Paragraphs / Chapters) 2) Audit Reports of the District Councils and other Panchayati Raj Institutions for the Revenue and Forest Department, the relevant areas c) Follow-up on the submission of explanatory memos on the Accounts Committee to the Accountants General c) Revenue, Civil and Commercial Reports c) Referral to the Public Accounts Committee to the Office of the Accountant General c) Action on the recommendations of the Public Accounts Committee c) Preparation of meetings / inspection of the Regional Office on pending accounts c) Preparation of the entire proceedings for the Principal Secretary c) Preparation of the Report to the Public Accounts Committee c) Preparation of the Report to the Public Accounts Committee c) Preparation of the Report.

Coordination: - A) Cost matters. Part I The following issues need special attention: - 1) Koyna earthquake. 2) Shortfall from 1970 to 1974. 3) Ratnagiri cyclone. 4) Drought from 1965 to 1968. 5) Village Extension Scheme. 6) Housing / Construction for landless agricultural labourers. 7) Rehabilitation of landless agricultural labourers. 8) Model Settlement Scheme. 9) Rehabilitation grants to former Inamdar / Shipyarders. 10) Housing advances to government employees under the control of Revenue and Forest Department. 11) Bonds given as per MS Tenure Ambolition Act. 12) Employees at Administrator and Collectorate Office, Ulhasnagar City. 13) Cash and Cash amount given to additional land holders as per the Agricultural Land Holding Ceiling Act. 14) Provision of additional land to the beneficiaries for land development and maintenance.

2) 229 - Land Revenue. 2) 230 - Stamp B Registration. 3) 235 - Collection of Taxes on Property Taxes and Ise Transactions. 4) 245 - Taxes and Duties on Goods and Services. 5) 363 - Gram Panchayats and Local Bodies. 248 - Appropriations to avoid / reduce FAN. 6) 249 - Interest, 7) 252 - Secretariat General Services. 8) 253 - District Administration. 9) 249 - Interest, 7) 252 - Secretariat General Services. 9) 255 - Police. 10) 259 - Public Works. 19) 265 - Other Administrative Services. 12) 268 - Complex and General Services. 13) 283 - Housing Construction. 14) 284 - Urban Development. 15) 288 Social Security and Welfare, 16) 289 - Assistance to Natural Disaster Victims. 17) 295 - Other Social Services. 304 - Recovery of Revenue Losses. 19) 305 - Agriculture. 20) 313 - Forests. 21) 363 - Additional Land Taxes and Duties on Subordinate Land Grants to Local Bodies. table_trans: e) Natural disaster relief and assistance. 4) Monitoring of sanctioned staff for audit under audit and follow-up for reimbursement of audit objections, etc. 5) Coordination at the Ministry level Coordination of work on annual audit of Revenue and Forest Department. Brief details of the above activities as suggested by the PAC-1 functionary are as follows. a) Follow-up of the balance sheet of accounting headings in Budget Estimates Part-I Revenue and Deposits and Part-II Expenditure to be maintained. b) Monitoring / Controlling the Internal Audit Team / Coordination Team created in the office under the Head of this Department to periodically review their work by visiting the respective offices. Reviewing and evaluating the work as reviewed, bringing to the notice of the Controlling Officers the defects / irregularities found. a) Monitoring the revenue report and follow-up of the compliance of the paragraphs, reporting the pending audit to the Audit Committee (Audit Committee) to follow-up on the audit reports.
